issues.message: Link is missing an accessible name

evidence.reason: Link has no usable text, aria-label, title, or image alt text.

issues.suggested_fix: Add descriptive link text that explains the link destination or action.

rule.title: Link missing accessible name
rule.why_it_matters: Screen reader users hear an empty or meaningless link and cannot decide whether to follow it.
rule.manual_review_notes: Links wrapping only images need useful alt text on the image.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static HTML cannot detect link names injected by JavaScript.

issues.message: Image is missing useful alt text

evidence.reason: Image is missing useful alt text.

issues.suggested_fix: Add alt text that describes the image purpose, or mark decorative images as presentation.

rule.title: Image missing useful alt text
rule.why_it_matters: Students using screen readers miss the image content entirely, which can hide instructions, diagrams, or required information.
rule.manual_review_notes: Confirm whether the image is informative or decorative; only a human can judge if existing alt text is actually useful.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static checks cannot judge alt text quality, only whether it exists.

issues.message: Page has multiple h1 headings

evidence.reason: Document has multiple h1 headings.

issues.suggested_fix: Use one main h1 for the page purpose, then organize sections with h2 and lower headings.

rule.title: Page has multiple h1 headings
rule.why_it_matters: Several h1 headings make it harder for students to identify the single main topic of the page.
rule.manual_review_notes: Some layouts intentionally use sectioned h1 elements; judge readability.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static HTML cannot evaluate how the headings are presented visually.

issues.message: Heading level is skipped

evidence.reason: Heading level jumps from h3 to h5.

issues.suggested_fix: Do not skip heading levels; move from h1 to h2 before h3.

rule.title: Heading level is skipped
rule.why_it_matters: Jumping from h1 to h3 breaks the page outline that screen reader users rely on to understand and navigate content.
rule.manual_review_notes: Check whether the heading order still makes sense when read as an outline.
rule.static_check_limitations: The check follows document order only; visual layout may differ.

issues.message: Focused control has no accessible name

evidence.reason: A keyboard user reached this control, but it has no label, text, aria-label, or other usable name.

issues.suggested_fix: Add a visible label, text content, or aria-label so students know what the control does.

rule.title: Focused control has no accessible name
rule.why_it_matters: A keyboard user can reach the control, but without a name a screen reader announces nothing useful about what it does.
rule.manual_review_notes: The accessible name is estimated; confirm with browser dev tools or a screen reader what is actually announced.
rule.browser_check_limitations: Accessible names are estimated from labels, text, and common attributes, not from a full accessibility tree computation.

issues.message: Focused control has no accessible name

evidence.reason: A keyboard user reached this control, but it has no label, text, aria-label, or other usable name.

issues.suggested_fix: Add a visible label, text content, or aria-label so students know what the control does.

rule.title: Focused control has no accessible name
rule.why_it_matters: A keyboard user can reach the control, but without a name a screen reader announces nothing useful about what it does.
rule.manual_review_notes: The accessible name is estimated; confirm with browser dev tools or a screen reader what is actually announced.
rule.browser_check_limitations: Accessible names are estimated from labels, text, and common attributes, not from a full accessibility tree computation.

issues.message: Keyboard focus landed on a hidden element

evidence.reason: This element received keyboard focus but does not appear to be visible on the page.

issues.suggested_fix: Remove hidden elements from the Tab order with tabindex="-1" or make them visible when focused.

rule.title: Keyboard focus landed on a hidden element
rule.why_it_matters: When focus moves to an invisible element, keyboard users lose track of where they are on the page.
rule.manual_review_notes: Check whether the element becomes visible on focus (like a skip link); that pattern is fine.
rule.browser_check_limitations: Visibility is estimated from element size and CSS and may not match what users actually see.

issues.message: Rendered text may have low contrast

evidence.reason: Computed foreground/background contrast is below the conservative 4.5:1 review threshold.

issues.suggested_fix: Increase foreground/background contrast and confirm the final design with manual review.

rule.title: Rendered text may have low contrast
rule.why_it_matters: Low-vision readers may not be able to read text when the computed foreground and background colors are too similar.
rule.manual_review_notes: Confirm the sampled colors match what users actually see, especially over gradients, images, and layered components.
rule.browser_check_limitations: The check samples browser-computed colors and does not prove full WCAG color contrast compliance.

issues.message: Fixed or wide content may prevent reflow

evidence.text: Skip to main content (Press Enter). SIGN IN Search Button Skip auxiliary navigat
evidence.reason: A rendered element is wider than the narrow viewport or uses a large fixed pixel width.

issues.suggested_fix: Replace fixed-width layout with responsive sizing such as max-width: 100%.

rule.title: Fixed or wide content may prevent reflow
rule.why_it_matters: Large fixed-width elements can force horizontal scrolling and hide content when users zoom or use narrow windows.
rule.manual_review_notes: Confirm whether the wide element is essential and whether all content remains reachable without two-dimensional scrolling.
rule.browser_check_limitations: The check flags obvious wide rendered elements and fixed pixel widths; it may miss complex layout problems.

rule.browser_check_limitations: The check flags obvious wide rendered elements and fixed pixel widths; it may miss complex layout problems.

issues.message: Focused element may not show a visible focus indicator

evidence.reason: The focused element did not expose an obvious outline, box-shadow, or border focus style in computed CSS.

issues.suggested_fix: Provide a clear :focus or :focus-visible style with visible outline, border, or shadow.

rule.title: Focused element may not show a visible focus indicator
rule.why_it_matters: Keyboard users with low vision need a visible indicator to know which control currently has focus.
rule.manual_review_notes: Manually tab through the page; some custom focus styles may be visible but not detected by this heuristic.
rule.browser_check_limitations: Focus indicator detection checks computed outline, border, and shadow heuristically and can miss custom visual treatments.

issues.message: Focused element may not show a visible focus indicator

issues.message: Focused element may not show a visible focus indicator

evidence.reason: The focused element did not expose an obvious outline, box-shadow, or border focus style in computed CSS.

issues.suggested_fix: Provide a clear :focus or :focus-visible style with visible outline, border, or shadow.

rule.title: Focused element may not show a visible focus indicator
rule.why_it_matters: Keyboard users with low vision need a visible indicator to know which control currently has focus.
rule.manual_review_notes: Manually tab through the page; some custom focus styles may be visible but not detected by this heuristic.
rule.browser_check_limitations: Focus indicator detection checks computed outline, border, and shadow heuristically and can miss custom visual treatments.

limitations: This prototype runs static HTML checks plus a basic keyboard interaction audit.
It does not replace a full human accessibility audit.
Browser mode does not simulate a full screen reader, and accessible names are estimated.
Low-vision browser checks are conservative heuristics and require manual review.

Accessible names are estimated and require manual review.
Focus overlay shows observed Tab stops in this browser run.
It does not represent every assistive technology experience.

limitations: Low-vision checks use browser-computed styles and conservative heuristics.
Rendered color contrast checks do not prove full WCAG conformance.
Zoom/reflow uses a narrow viewport approximation rather than a full zoom compliance test.
Focus indicator detection may miss custom focus styles.

ai_scout.summary: Possible accessibility barriers found on the AHEAD website, including missing link names and image alt text, which may impact users with disabilities.

ai_suggested_observations.observation: Links may be missing accessible names, potentially causing navigation difficulties for users relying on assistive technologies.
ai_suggested_observations.why_it_may_matter: Accessible link names are crucial for screen reader users and those who rely on keyboard navigation to understand the purpose of links.
ai_suggested_observations.related_deterministic_evidence: Multiple instances of missing link names were detected, such as in the snippets <a id="MainCopy_ctl05_BlogList_ProfileLink_0" href="https://www.ahead.org/people/ahead"> and <a href="http://accessinghigherground.org/">.

ai_suggested_observations.observation: An image may be missing useful alt text, which could hinder users who rely on screen readers or have low vision from understanding the content.
ai_suggested_observations.why_it_may_matter: Alt text provides a description of images for users who cannot see them, ensuring equal access to information.
ai_suggested_observations.related_deterministic_evidence: An image with an empty alt attribute was found: .

ai_suggested_observations.observation: The page structure may have skipped heading levels, potentially causing confusion for users navigating the content using assistive technologies.
ai_suggested_observations.why_it_may_matter: Consistent heading levels help users understand the structure and hierarchy of the page content.
ai_suggested_observations.related_deterministic_evidence: Heading level jumps from h3 to h5 were detected, as indicated by snippets like <h5>.

outreach_notes: We suggest reviewing the website for accessibility barriers, particularly focusing on link names, image alt text, and heading structure, to ensure an inclusive experience for all users.
limitations: AI Scout findings are suggestions and need human review.
AI Scout output is not a confirmed accessibility finding.
AI Scout does not determine legal compliance, WCAG compliance, or screen-reader behavior.
AI Scout output is not a needs human review accessibility finding.
